results.possibleIdentity: Brian D. King was an electronic engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, noted for his expertise in circuit design and control systems. He earned a Master of Science in Electrical Engineering from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) in 1960, with a thesis titled "The design for an accurate audio oscillator." His work involved the application of sampled data feedback and digitalized reference voltages to frequency selection, which he noted were particularly useful for psychophysical experiments. Based in New York, he was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization facilitating collaborations between artists and technical professionals. His technical skills in electronic circuits and control systems made him a relevant collaborator for experimental projects in the art-science community during that era.
